title: 10 precautions to take when using Silybum marianum (Milk Thistle) for medicinal purposes
intro: When using Silybum marianum, or milk thistle, for medicinal purposes, it is important to take certain precautions. First, it is essential to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen, including milk thistle. This is especially important for individuals who are pregnant or breastfeeding, as the safety of milk thistle in these populations has not been established. Additionally, milk thistle may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, and can have adverse effects when taken in large doses or for extended periods. Therefore, it is crucial to follow the recommended dosage and duration of use provided by a healthcare professional. Lastly, while milk thistle is generally considered safe, some people may experience mild side effects, such as stomach upset or diarrhea, and should discontinue use if these symptoms persist or worsen.
